title: Arch spring footwear system for providing energy-return during human gait
abstract: A footwear system utilizing a configuration of components or sub-assembles is provided to promote energy-return features during human gait. The footwear system configuration utilizes structural arches and cantilever beams formed and/or fabricated with structural materials allowing for storage and release of kinetic energy produced during gait motions. The energy transfer is enhanced by a forward propulsion component during gait while providing cushioning via elastic deformation of the various semi-rigid components comprising the system.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A footwear system provided with a footwear article for returning energy to aid in the propulsion of human gait, comprising: a heel component including a cantilevered extension and an arcuate structure extending to the cantilevered extension to facilitate energy-return; and a toe arch component including a saddle structure to distribute energy return from the heel component. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein at least the heel component and the toe arch component are constructed of a composite material. 3. The system of claim 2, wherein the composite material allowing flexural deformation to bend and store elastic energy. 4. The system of claim 1, wherein at least the heel component and the toe arch component are constructed of an injected plastic material. 5. The system of claim 1, wherein at least the heel component and the toe arch component are constructed of a metallic spring material. 6. The system of claim 1, wherein the footwear article is a shoe body. 7. The system of claim 6, further including an interfacing attachment portion securing the shoe body with the heel component and the toe arch component and allowing for non- restrictive flexion of the footwear system. 8. The system of claim 7, wherein the interfacing attachment portion includes a device selected from the group consisting of: one or more straps, one or more adjustable ties, and one or more clamps. 9. The system of claim 1, wherein the toe arch component includes an arcuate arch structure. 10. The system of claim 9, wherein the arcuate arch structure includes a plurality of extension segments. 11. The system of claim 10, wherein the plurality of extension segments includes a first arch segment to provide medial arch support and a second arch segment to provide lateral arch support. 12. The system of claim 11, wherein a gap is define between the first arch segment and the second arch segment. 13. The system of claim 9, wherein the arcuate arch structure curves under the toe arch component. 14. The system of claim 1, wherein the cantilevered extension and the arcuate structure combine to define a generally C-shaped construct. 15. The system of claim 1, wherein at least the heel component and the toe arch component are provided internally within the footwear article. 16. The system of claim 1, wherein at least the heel component and the toe arch component are provided external to the footwear article. 17. A footwear system provided with a footwear article for returning energy to aid in the propulsion of human gait, comprising: a heel component including a cantilevered extension and an arcuate structure extending to the cantilevered extension to facilitate energy-return; and a toe arch component including a saddle structure and an arcuate arch structure, with the toe arch component in operable communication with the heel component. 18. The system of claim 17, wherein the heel component and the toe arch component are constructed of a material selected from the group consisting of: a composite, a plastic material, and a metallic spring material. 19. The system of claim 17, further including an interfacing attachment portion including a device selected from the group consisting of: one or more straps, one or more adjustable ties, and one or more clamps. 20. The system of claim 17, wherein the cantilevered extension and the arcuate structure combine to define a generally C-shaped construct.
